title: W7.3 — MakerRebateModel
pyright_warnings_note: uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/paper/cost.py and tests/paper/test_maker_rebate.py. The 0-error count is the W7.3 pyright --strict requirement. The existing ~700 informational warnings are demoted per pyrightconfig.json.

pytest_regression.note: Baseline 529 reflects the W3 + W5 + W6 + W7.1 + W7.2 batch state. W7.3 adds 2 tests: test_limit_order_gets_rebate, test_taker_order_no_rebate. 529 + 2 = 531 passed, 16 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions.

maker_rebate_model.name: MakerRebateModel
maker_rebate_model.config_class: RebateConfig (frozen dataclass with rebate_bps=0.2, cost: CostModel | None = None)

public_methods.name: net_cost_bps
public_methods.description: Returns the net cost in bps of notional. 'limit' => commission_bps - rebate_bps; 'market' => commission_bps (no rebate). Unknown order_kind raises ValueError.

public_methods.name: net_cost_cash
public_methods.description: Returns the net cost in cash units: notional * net_cost_bps(order_kind) / 1e4. Matches the v1 CostModel.total_cost convention. Negative notional raises ValueError.

public_properties.name: rebate_bps
public_properties.description: The per-side rebate for limit orders, in bps of notional. Default 0.2 (the v1 BTC venue default for the maker programme).

public_properties.name: commission_bps
public_properties.description: The commission read from the underlying CostModel. Returns 0.0 when no CostModel is configured (the v1 contract for tests that don't care about commission).

test_evidence.test_limit_order_gets_rebate: 1 BTC limit order at 50,000 USDT with commission_bps=10, rebate_bps=0.2: net cost = 9.8 bps; cash cost = 50_000 * 9.8 / 1e4 = 49.0 USDT.
test_evidence.test_taker_order_no_rebate: 1 BTC market order at 50,000 USDT with commission_bps=10, rebate_bps=0.2: net cost = 10 bps; cash cost = 50_000 * 10 / 1e4 = 50.0 USDT. The differential between market and limit is exactly the rebate (0.2 bps, or 1.0 USDT in cash for a 50,000 USDT notional).

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/paper/cost.py created with RebateConfig, MakerRebateModel, and OrderKind (Literal['limit', 'market']). The module is pure: no IO, no async, no global state. The v1 contract is (rebate_bps=0.2, cost=CostModel(commission_bps=10.0)). The model is a thin additive layer over CostModel; the v1 CostModel contract is unchanged.

tests/paper/test_maker_rebate.py::test_limit_order_gets_rebate passes. With commission_bps=10 and rebate_bps=0.2, a 1 BTC limit order at 50,000 USDT produces a net cost of (10 - 0.2) = 9.8 bps and a cash cost of 49.0 USDT. The rebate is positive (the trader is paid to add liquidity), so the limit order's net cost is strictly less than the commission.

tests/paper/test_maker_rebate.py::test_taker_order_no_rebate passes. With commission_bps=10 and rebate_bps=0.2, a 1 BTC market order at 50,000 USDT produces a net cost of 10 bps (no rebate) and a cash cost of 50.0 USDT. The market-limit differential is exactly the rebate (0.2 bps). Defensive: unknown order_kind ('stop') raises ValueError; negative notional raises ValueError.
uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/paper/cost.py and tests/paper/test_maker_rebate.py. The 0-error count is the W7.3 pyright --strict requirement.

uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 531 passed, 16 skipped, 0 failed (baseline 529 + 2 new W7.3 tests, 0 regressions). The maker_rebate module is additive to the paper package; the v1 CostModel contract is unchanged.

deviation_from_spec.rebate_source_is_cost_model: The plan says the rebate model is a thin layer that takes the commission from the existing CostModel. The implementation reads commission_bps from the supplied CostModel (configurable via the RebateConfig.cost field; default None means zero commission). This preserves the v1 CostModel contract (W2 calibration, W3 backtest, W6 sizer) — the maker rebate is consumed at the paper-trader level, not at the backtest level.

notes: W7.3 ships the MakerRebateModel with a +0.2 bps rebate for limit orders and 0 bps for market (taker) orders. The net cost is commission_bps - rebate_bps for limit orders; commission_bps for market orders. The 2 new tests pin the W7.3 acceptance criteria: limit order rebate (1 BTC at 50,000 USDT with commission=10 bps, rebate=0.2 bps produces net=9.8 bps and cash=49.0 USDT), and taker no-rebate (same order as a market produces net=10 bps and cash=50.0 USDT). The module is pure and additive; the v1 CostModel contract is unchanged. The paper trader can now wire the maker rebate into the order submission path to model venue-specific maker-taker fee schedules.
